decisions.question: Event loop architecture: how should the dashboard interleave keyboard event handling and periodic session-file polling in a synchronous (no async runtime) Rust binary? Options include: (A) single-threaded tick loop using crossterm::event::poll() with a short timeout, (B) two threads — one for keyboard events, one for file polling — communicating via a channel, (C) non-blocking crossterm::event::try_read() with an explicit sleep between ticks.

Session state model: how should the dashboard represent the session hierarchy in memory, derive aggregate counts, and refresh state on each poll cycle? Options include: (A) read all session headers on each tick and rebuild the tree from scratch, (B) cache session metadata by last-modified time and re-read only changed files, (C) maintain a mutable tree ADT with incremental diffs applied on each poll cycle.

ratatui widget layout: how should the TUI panels be organized? Options include: (A) single scrollable session list occupying the full window with a contextual detail footer (gate + evidence) for the selected row, (B) vertical split with session tree on the left (~60%) and detail panel on the right (~40%), (C) tabbed layout with a 'Sessions' tab and a 'Detail' tab that the user switches between.

Module organization: where should dashboard code live in the existing koto codebase? Options include: (A) new file src/cli/dashboard.rs (alongside other CLI modules like next.rs, session.rs), (B) new top-level module src/dashboard/ with its own mod.rs, data layer, and rendering submodules, (C) inline in src/cli/mod.rs alongside the Dashboard command handler.
